solution:
Correct order should be :    ii  <  i  <  iv < iii

(i) N is sp3 hybridised, 1°Amine without delocalization.

(ii) N is sp2 hybridised.

(iii) Guanidine is a stronger base than amidine (resonance stabilised)

(iv) 2° Amine, N is sp3 hybridised.

question:

(A) Primary amines do not give diazonium salts when treated with $\mathrm{NaNO}_2$ in acidic condition.
(B) Aliphatic and aromatic primary amines on heating with $\mathrm{CHCl}_3$ and ethanolic KOH form carbylamines.
(C) Secondary and tertiary amines also give carbylamine test.
(D) Benzenesulfonyl chloride is known as Hinsberg's reagent.
(E) Tertiary amines reacts with benzenesulfonyl chloride very easily.

solution: (C) Only primary amine gives carbyl amine test (D) Benzene sulphonyl chloride $\mathrm{Ph}-\mathrm{SO}_2 \mathrm{Cl}$ $\qquad$ Hinsberg reagent (E) Tertiary amine do not react with $\mathrm{Ph}-\mathrm{SO}_2 \mathrm{Cl}$ So correct options are (B) and (D) only

<math><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>></mo><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>></mo><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>></mo><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">V</mi></mrow></mfenced></math>

<math><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>></mo><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>></mo><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">V</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>></mo><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi></mrow></mfenced></math>

<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>></mo><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>></mo><mfenced separators="|"><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi></mfenced><mo>></mo><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">V</mi></mrow></mfenced></math>
solution:
Basic strength depends upon availability of lone pairs. Greater the resonance of lone pairs lesser the basic strength. In (II) and (III) lone pair is localised. (III) is more basic due to +I effect of carbon chain whereas in II, negative mesomeric effect is operating. In (I) and (IV) lone pair is delocalised, so their basic strength is less that former two amines. If (IV) donates its lone pair then its aromaticity will get lost therefore, its basic strength is less than that of I.

Overall order of basic strength is  III>II>I>IV

q: The correct order of basic nature on aqueous solution for the bases \(\mathrm{NH}_3, \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{~N}-\mathrm{NH}_2, \mathrm{CH}_3 \mathrm{CH}_2 \mathrm{NH}_2\), \(\left(\mathrm{CH}_3 \mathrm{CH}_2\right)_2 \mathrm{NH}\) and \(\left(\mathrm{CH}_3 \mathrm{CH}_2\right)_3 \mathrm{~N}\) is :
question: The correct order of basic nature on aqueous solution for the bases \(\mathrm{NH}_3, \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{~N}-\mathrm{NH}_2, \mathrm{CH}_3 \mathrm{CH}_2 \mathrm{NH}_2\), \(\left(\mathrm{CH}_3 \mathrm{CH}_2\right)_2 \mathrm{NH}\) and \(\left(\mathrm{CH}_3 \mathrm{CH}_2\right)_3 \mathrm{~N}\) is :
solution:
Basic strength of amine depends on hydrogen bonding and electronic inductive effect.
\(\mathrm{NH}(\mathrm{Et})_2 \gt \mathrm{N}(\mathrm{Et})_3 \gt \mathrm{NH}_2 \mathrm{Et} \gt \mathrm{\stackrel{\cdot \cdot}{N}H}_3 \gt \mathrm{NH}_2-\mathrm{NH}_2\)

q:
Match List I with List II

List I (Amines) List II pKb
A. Aniline I. 3.25
B. Ethanamine II. 3.00
C. N-Ethylethanamine III. 9.38
D. N, N-Diethylethanamine IV. 3.29

Choose the correct answer from the options given below:-
question:
Match List I with List II

List I (Amines) List II pKb
A. Aniline I. 3.25
B. Ethanamine II. 3.00
C. N-Ethylethanamine III. 9.38
D. N, N-Diethylethanamine IV. 3.29

Choose the correct answer from the options given below:-
solution:
Among given aliphatic amines, 2° amine is most basic, followed by 3° amine and 1° amine.

Therefore, the correct basic strength (Kb) order of the given amines is

(C) > (D) > (B) > (A)

The pKb order of the given amines will be just the opposite of their basic strength order.

The order is (A) > (B) > (D) > (C)

So the correct answer is A-III, B-IV, C-II, D-I

(B)
 

(C)
 

(D)
 

Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
solution:
When benzyl bromide is treated with AgCN, it forms benzyl isocyanide. This reaction is followed by the SN2 mechanism.

Benzylamine on heating with chloroform and ethanolic potassium hydroxide forms Benzyl isocyanides.  This reaction is known as carbylamine reaction or isocyanide test.

2° amine does not give carbylamine reaction.

 So, the correct option is C.

subject: Chemistry
q:
Consider the following reactions sequence

When the product (P) is subjected to Carius analysis using $AgNO_3$, $1.0$ g of the product (P) will produce _______ g of the precipitate of AgBr. (Nearest Integer)
(Given: molar mass in g mol$^{-1}$ C : 12, H : 1, O : 16, N : 14, Br : 80, Ag : 108)
question:
Consider the following reactions sequence

When the product (P) is subjected to Carius analysis using $AgNO_3$, $1.0$ g of the product (P) will produce _______ g of the precipitate of AgBr. (Nearest Integer)
(Given: molar mass in g mol$^{-1}$ C : 12, H : 1, O : 16, N : 14, Br : 80, Ag : 108)
solution:
The given reactant is p-nitrotoluene.

Step (i): Reduction with Sn/HCl followed by $OH^-$ converts the $-NO_2$ group to an $-NH_2$ group, yielding p-toluidine.

Step (ii): Reaction with acetic anhydride, $(CH_3CO)_2O$, protects the amino group by forming N-(4-methylphenyl)acetamide.

Step (iii): Bromination with $Br_2/AlBr_3$ directs the incoming bromine to the ortho position relative to the strongly activating $-NHCOCH_3$ group (the para position is blocked by the methyl group). This yields 2-bromo-4-methylacetanilide.

Step (iv): Acid hydrolysis with $H_3O^+$ removes the acetyl group, yielding 2-bromo-4-methylaniline as the major product (P).

The molecular formula of product P (2-bromo-4-methylaniline) is $C_7H_8NBr$.
Molar mass of P = $(7 \times 12) + (8 \times 1) + 14 + 80 = 186 \text{ g mol}^{-1}$.

In Carius analysis, the bromine in the organic compound is quantitatively converted to silver bromide (AgBr).
Molar mass of AgBr = $108 + 80 = 188 \text{ g mol}^{-1}$.

Since one molecule of P contains one bromine atom, 1 mole of P produces 1 mole of AgBr.
Moles of P in $1.0 \text{ g} = \dfrac{1.0}{186} \text{ mol}$.
Moles of AgBr produced = $\dfrac{1.0}{186} \text{ mol}$.
Mass of AgBr produced = $\dfrac{1.0}{186} \times 188 \text{ g} \approx 1.01 \text{ g}$.

Rounding to the nearest integer, the mass of the AgBr precipitate is 1 g.

Answer: $1$
